video_title: Devil May Cry | Opening Credits | Netflix
comment_text:
Yes, the visuals were absolutely stunning. The animation, fight choreography, dark aesthetic — all of it was on point. I won’t deny that.
But unfortunately, that's where the praise ends.

The biggest problem?
Dante wasn’t the main character. In fact, it felt like he was pushed to the side completely — emotionally flat, underdeveloped, and nearly silent throughout the entire series.
How is this a Devil May Cry adaptation when the very soul of the franchise — Dante himself — is reduced to a background character?

Instead, Merid took up most of the spotlight.
Sure, she might have her own interesting arc, but it should’ve been told in a separate season or DLC-style spinoff, not at the expense of Dante’s presence. This story didn’t feel like it was about him at all.

I was expecting a story about Dante — his past, his internal conflicts, his purpose.
But what we got was a flashy side story where the protagonist is barely involved.
As a long-time fan of the DMC universe, this left me disconnected and frustrated.

video_title: Devil May Cry | Opening Credits | Netflix
comment_text:
I used to think it's funny how Capcom can't seem to ever get a decent adaptation made of any of their games, but after watching the first couple episodes of this it's just starting to get sad. It's almost impressive how lame it is. Like an unpleasant hybrid of old-school DMC, the reboot and a whole slew of unnecessary fan fiction. Dante himself is OK, but everything else is just... why? Especially how they almost aggressively are throwing Lady's character under a bus. 

Also, them trying to make DMC take place in the same universe as RE while also constantly making real-world references is messing with my head.

All that said, it's better than the Dragon's Dogma show and most of the horrible RE movies (LA and animated) but I still see no reason to actually finish it. Life's too short.
